meta.title: NebulaDB
meta.author: Mauricio Gomez
meta.description: The database your team actually wants to use. A pitch deck for our Series A.

slides.title: NebulaDB
slides.subtitle: The multi-model database for the next generation of applications

slides.title: Teams run 3–5 databases just to ship one app
slides.subtitle: Postgres for relational. Redis for caching. Elasticsearch for search. MongoDB for documents. S3 for files.

items: Infra complexity: each DB needs provisioning, backups, monitoring, and expertise
Data sync nightmares: keeping search indexes in sync with the primary store
Developer friction: 4 different query languages, 4 different mental models
Cost explosion: paying for 4 managed services that mostly sit idle
Compliance burden: data scattered across systems, each with different retention policies

blocks.content: There are over 350 active database management systems. The average engineering team uses 3.7 of them.

slides.title: NebulaDB: One database. Every model.
slides.subtitle: Relational, document, graph, vector, key-value, and full-text search — in a single engine with one query language.

items: Relational: Full SQL with ACID transactions, joins, and constraints
Document: JSON-native storage with indexing on nested paths
Vector: HNSW indices for embeddings, built-in, no plugin needed
Graph: Cypher-compatible graph traversal engine
Full-text: BM25 + neural hybrid search out of the box
Key-value: Sub-millisecond reads with Redis-compatible wire protocol
4 separate databases to manage
4 different query languages
Data sync pipelines everywhere
4x the infra cost
SRE team required
One database. Every model.
One query language (NSQL)
No sync — everything in one engine
1/4 the infra cost
Managed or self-hosted

blocks.content: LTV:CAC ratio of 12.2x in the self-serve tier. Enterprise CAC payback under 4 months.

slides.title: Series A — $8M
slides.subtitle: We are raising to scale our cloud platform and build the team.

items: Expand the core engine team (3 senior distributed systems engineers)
Build managed cloud offering on AWS, GCP, and Azure
Launch enterprise sales motion with dedicated solutions architects
Ship the VS Code extension and CLI tooling
Build the community: conferences, meetups, and documentation

blocks.content: ✨ Fun fact: Our largest open-source contributor is a 17-year-old in Brazil who's never worked at a database company.

slides.subtitle: — A real Slack message from a paying customer

blocks.content: We just deleted three database instances and migrated 40 microservices to NebulaDB. Our infra bill dropped by 60% and our on-call alerts dropped to zero. This is the most impactful infrastructure change we've made in 3 years.

slides.title: Thank you
slides.subtitle: Let's build the database that developers deserve.
